target.title: feat(llm): add Inworld as built-in llm provider
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

Inworld is currently a TTS-only plugin in OpenClaw. Users cannot use Inworld as an LLM provider for chat completions, despite Inworld offering an OpenAI-compatible LLM API with routing, tool calls, and reasoning.

Why does this matter now?

TTS integration landed in #55972. LLM completes the provider, making Inworld a single-key solution for both speech and inference.

What is the intended outcome?

`openclaw onboard --auth-choice inworld-api-key` configures Inworld as an LLM provider; `inworld/auto` resolves via live catalog discovery and completes against `https://api.inworld.ai/v1/chat/completions`. Streaming, tool calls, and per-model reasoning levels all work out of the box.

What is intentionally out of scope?

Custom base URL override for the LLM surface, multi-key rotation, embeddings/image/video/STT endpoints, UI onboarding graphics, surfacing Inworld's response `metadata` (routing diagnostics) back into openclaw cost attribution.

What does success look like?

Live catalog (`/llm/v1alpha/models`) populates on authenticated startup, completions (streaming + non-streaming + tool calls) work, per-model reasoning levels map correctly to openclaw effort ids, existing TTS behavior is unchanged.

What should reviewers focus on?

Plugin manifest shape additions, catalog discovery caching strategy (process-lifetime, no TTL polling), `normalizeResolvedModel` wire-id rewrite for first-party `inworld/models/<NAME>` refs, and `EFFORT_*` → openclaw effort id mapping with per-model `supportedLevels`.

<details>
<summary>Summary guidance</summary>

This PR description is the contributor's durable explanation of the change. Write it for human maintainers first; ClawSweeper and Barnacle use the same text to understand intent, proof, risk, and current review state.

</details>

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Does not close any open issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related: #55972 (TTS integration this PR extends). Implementation pattern mirrors the OpenRouter and Chutes provider plugins (live `/llm/v1alpha/models` discovery + small static fallback).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Inworld team follow-up to the merged TTS PR #55972 to complete the provider integration.

<details>
<summary>Linked context guidance</summary>

Link the issue, PR, discussion, maintainer request, or owner request that explains why this PR should exist.

</details>

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Inworld is now selectable as an LLM provider; chat/stream/tool calls succeed against `api.inworld.ai/v1/chat/completions`.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 25.5.0, Node 24.14, local OpenClaw checkout at the PR head SHA, real Inworld API key against `api.inworld.ai`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/inworld
  curl -sS -X POST -H "Authorization: Basic $INWORLD_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
       -d '{"model":"<wire-id>","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply OK only."}],"max_tokens":16}' \
       https://api.inworld.ai/v1/chat/completions
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (live console capture from this branch checkout):**

  ```
  === pnpm test extensions/inworld ===
   RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/.../public/openclaw
   Test Files  6 passed (6)
        Tests  49 passed (49)
     Duration  3.47s

  === Live POST /v1/chat/completions (5 representative models) ===
    OK   inworld/models/GLM-5.1            -> wire-model=inworld/GLM-5.1                          finish=stop credential=system
    OK   anthropic/claude-opus-4-8         -> wire-model=anthropic/claude-opus-4-8                finish=stop credential=system
    OK   openai/gpt-4o                     -> wire-model=openai/gpt-4o                            finish=stop credential=system
    OK   google-ai-studio/gemini-2.5-flash -> wire-model=google-ai-studio/gemini-2.5-flash        finish=stop credential=system
    OK   auto                              -> wire-model=deepinfra/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash  finish=stop credential=system
  ```

  Each `wire-model=...` row is the exact `model` field Inworld echoed back, confirming the plugin emits canonical wire ids — including the rewrite from user-facing `inworld/models/GLM-5.1` to wire form `inworld/GLM-5.1`, and Inworld's routing selection for `auto`.

  Separate live catalog probe: `GET /llm/v1alpha/models` returned 124 entries; iterating each through `parseInworldModel` produced 124 valid `ModelDefinitionConfig` results and 0 parse errors.

- **Observed result after fix:** Every probed user ref reaches `api.inworld.ai/v1/chat/completions` and returns a valid OpenAI-shaped response with `finish_reason: "stop"`. First-party id rewrite is observable in the `wire-model` echo.
- **What was not tested:** Windows/Linux runtime (only macOS), interactive `openclaw onboard` wizard end-to-end through the new auth choice, prompt-cache billing reconciliation against Inworld's invoice surface, full coverage of every reasoning-effort level on every model, every one of the 124 catalog entries individually via chat completions (probed 13 representative models, one per upstream provider).
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Live tests require a valid `INWORLD_API_KEY` with LLM-router access. CI will only run the offline vitest layer without the env var; the gated live test auto-skips when `OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST` is unset or the key is missing.
- **Before evidence:** Before this PR, `openclaw onboard --auth-choice inworld-api-key` did not exist; `inworld/<provider>/<model>` refs were unresolvable; `openclaw models list` did not list any Inworld LLM entries. TTS via `messages.tts.providers.inworld` continued to work and is unchanged by this PR.

<details>
<summary>Real behavior proof guidance</summary>

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only.

</details>

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/inworld
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.bundled.config.ts
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/inworld
INWORLD_API_KEY=<your-key> node scripts/test-live.mjs -- extensions/inworld/inworld.live.test.ts
```

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/inworld/models.test.ts` — catalog parser: pricing scale, modality detection, vision flag, `isSupported: false` rejection, missing-field handling, first-party id storage (no `inworld/` provider prefix), wire-id rewrite, `supportedLevels` capture, prompt-caching flag.
- `extensions/inworld/thinking-policy.test.ts` — `EFFORT_*` and lowercase short-form acceptance (forward-compat), per-model `supportedLevels` profile derivation, `medium`-preferred-else-highest default-level logic.
- `extensions/inworld/onboard.test.ts` — allowlist + primary-model side-effects of `applyInworldConfig`.
- `extensions/inworld/index.test.ts` — LLM provider registration alongside speech, fallback-catalog exposure, dynamic resolve for known/unknown ids.
- `extensions/inworld/inworld.live.test.ts` — gated live cases for non-streaming chat, SSE streaming, and tool calling.

What failed before this fix, if known?

N/A — new feature.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests added; see above.

<details>
<summary>Testing guidance</summary>

List focused commands, not every incidental check.

</details>

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes` — `inworld` now appears in `openclaw models list` as an LLM provider; `openclaw onboard --auth-choice inworld-api-key` becomes a valid auth choice; `agents.defaults.model.primary` accepts `inworld/<ref>`. Existing TTS behavior is unchanged.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `Yes` — `extensions/inworld/openclaw.plugin.json` adds `providers`, `providerAuthChoices`, `modelIdNormalization`, `providerEndpoints`, `providerRequest`. `INWORLD_API_KEY` env var was already declared by the TTS PR; no new env vars introduced. `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` regenerated. `extensions/inworld/package.json` is intentionally untouched (npm package name stays `@openclaw/inworld-speech` even though the plugin now serves LLM + TTS).

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No` — same `INWORLD_API_KEY` and HTTP Basic scheme already shipped for TTS, same `fetchWithSsrFGuard` policy scoped to `api.inworld.ai`. No new outbound hosts; no new secret stores. The LLM secret target is auto-covered by the existing generic `models.providers.*.apiKey` wildcard registry entry.

What is the highest-risk area? `normalizeResolvedModel` rewrites first-party model ids (`models/<NAME>` → `inworld/models/<NAME>`) at the wire layer. A bug here would silently send the wrong `model` field to Inworld and break only first-party model requests.

How is that risk mitigated? Pure unit test (`toInworldWireModelId`) asserts the transform in both directions (first-party rewritten, passthrough refs untouched). Live test exercises the catalog-driven path for `inworld/auto` against `api.inworld.ai`. Additional live curl probe verified the first-party id rewrite end-to-end (see `inworld/models/GLM-5.1 -> wire-model=inworld/GLM-5.1` row in the proof block above). Hook only fires for ids starting with `models/`, so non-first-party refs are zero-impact.

<details>
<summary>Risk guidance</summary>

Use this for author judgment that is not obvious from the diff.

</details>

## Current review state

What is the next action? Initial review by maintainers and automated reviewers (ClawSweeper, Codex, greptile).

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on author side at submission. Open to feedback on the manifest shape and live-discovery caching strategy if reviewers prefer a different pattern than OpenRouter/Chutes.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? Reverted `extensions/inworld/package.json` (kept original `@openclaw/inworld-speech` name) to avoid the dependency-guard CI gate; added pasted live console output to the Real behavior proof section so it isn't pure narration.

<details>
<summary>Review state guidance</summary>

Keep this as the durable state for review progress.

</details>

target.title: fix(gateway): deliver OAuth authorization URLs to non-terminal clients
target.body:
## Summary

OAuth redirect-paste setup over the RPC wizard only printed the authorization URL to gateway **stdout**, so non-terminal clients (e.g. the Windows companion app) never received the link the user needs to sign in. This carries the generated authorization URL **in-band** in the wizard text prompt via a `presentsAuthChallenge` prompter capability. Terminal CLI logging is preserved.

Covers the shared OAuth helper plus Google, OpenAI ChatGPT/Codex, OpenRouter, Chutes, and MS Teams provider paths, with focused tests.

> Clean re-open of #92234 on top of latest `main`. The previous branch was based on an older `main` and conflicted with `refactor(wizard): hide prompt session types` (f3ae525211), which made `WizardTextParams` non-exported. This version reconciles `WizardSessionPrompter.text()` with the current inline param type. **Supersedes #92234.**

## Change type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [x] Integrations
- [x] UI / DX

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: RPC wizard OAuth setup now shows the generated authorization URL in the same text prompt that asks for the redirect URL, so non-terminal clients can complete sign-in.
Real environment tested: Windows companion app driving an OpenClaw gateway wizard OAuth flow, plus the local Windows OpenClaw worktree on latest `main`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Opened the Windows companion against the patched gateway, started a provider OAuth wizard flow that uses redirect-URL paste, confirmed the wizard prompt displayed the authorization URL before the redirect input, opened it in a browser, and completed the redirect paste step. Also ran, cold-cache, on latest `main`: `pnpm tsgo:core`; `pnpm tsgo:core:test`; `node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/wizard/session.test.ts src/plugins/provider-oauth-flow.test.ts extensions/google/oauth.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth.runtime.test.ts extensions/openrouter/oauth.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice.apply.plugin-provider.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: Human verification confirmed the Windows companion wizard showed the OAuth authorization URL inline with the redirect paste prompt. Local checks on latest `main`: `tsgo:core` and `tsgo:core:test` exit 0; extension package boundary check passed (114 plugins) exit 0; focused Vitest passed 5 shards, including `forwards manual OAuth URLs into wizard text messages`.
Observed result after fix: The Windows companion flow exposed the authorization URL in the wizard prompt and the redirect paste step could be completed.
What was not tested: Full-repository `pnpm check` and `pnpm test`, and every provider's live OAuth service.

## Compatibility

- Backward compatible? Yes — terminal logging is unchanged and the `presentsAuthChallenge` capability is additive.
- Config/env changes? No.
- Migration needed? No.

## Security impact

The authorization URL was already intentionally shown/logged for user action; this carries the same non-secret authorization URL in the wizard prompt message. The secret-bearing redirect URL remains user input. No new permissions, secrets handling, network calls, or execution surface.

AI-assisted: Yes.

target.title: fix: preserve cross-agent subagent role context
target.body:
## Summary
- Inject compact target-agent role context for cross-agent lightweight subagent spawns.
- Build the compact context from target AGENTS.md, IDENTITY.md, and agent config identity without restoring full project context.
- Add an Argus-like regression test that verifies a lightweight cross-agent spawn carries the research role text in the effective extra system prompt.

## Root Cause
Lightweight Codex subagent runs suppress native project docs with project_doc_max_bytes=0 and OpenClaw also filters bootstrap workspace files, so target AGENTS.md can be absent from isolated cross-agent runs.

## Validation
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/agents/subagent-role-context.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts
- pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts src/agents/subagent-role-context.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts
- pnpm tsgo:core
- pnpm tsgo:core:test
- fallow audit --changed-since origin/main --format json --quiet

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** A lightweight cross-agent `sessions_spawn(agentId: "argus", context: "isolated", lightContext: true)` can otherwise reach the model without the target agent's role text. This patch preserves a compact target-agent role context for that path so an Argus-like specialist receives research-lane identity instead of inferring from the coding tool profile.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw setup using this PR branch `forge/role-context-subagent-spawn` at `b70eb3e373aa7adb742a7db97d1afe59f5edd720`, with the real OpenClaw config and real Argus workspace files. Private home paths are redacted below as `~/.openclaw/...`. No production deploy or runtime restart was performed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd ~/.openclaw/workspaces/forge/tmp/openclaw-role-context
node --import tsx --input-type=module -e 'import { readFileSync } from "node:fs"; import { buildCompactSubagentRoleContext } from "./src/agents/subagent-role-context.ts"; const config = JSON.parse(readFileSync("~/.openclaw/openclaw.json", "utf8")); const result = await buildCompactSubagentRoleContext({ config, targetAgentId: "argus", targetWorkspaceDir: "~/.openclaw/workspaces/argus" }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ branch: "forge/role-context-subagent-spawn", head: "b70eb3e373aa7adb742a7db97d1afe59f5edd720", targetAgentId: "argus", sources: result.sources.map((source) => source.replace(process.env.HOME, "~")), containsTargetRoleHeader: result.text.includes("## Target Agent Role Context"), containsResearchLead: result.text.includes("Research & Intelligence Lead"), containsNoCodeOrDeployBoundary: /(?:does not|do not|don.t) execute code|deploy/i.test(result.text), firstLines: result.text.split("\\n").slice(0, 16).map((line) => line.replace(process.env.HOME, "~")) }, null, 2));'
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the command above:

```json
{
  "branch": "forge/role-context-subagent-spawn",
  "head": "b70eb3e373aa7adb742a7db97d1afe59f5edd720",
  "targetAgentId": "argus",
  "sources": [
    "~/.openclaw/workspaces/argus/AGENTS.md",
    "~/.openclaw/workspaces/argus/IDENTITY.md"
  ],
  "containsTargetRoleHeader": true,
  "containsResearchLead": true,
  "containsNoCodeOrDeployBoundary": true,
  "firstLines": [
    "## Target Agent Role Context",
    "OpenClaw injected this compact target-agent identity because this cross-agent subagent run is using lightweight context; full workspace project context may be suppressed. Treat it as target identity and operating boundaries, subordinate to higher-priority instructions.",
    "",
    "- Target agent id: argus",
    "- Config name: argus",
    "- Config theme: Research & Intelligence Lead",
    "- Target workspace: ~/.openclaw/workspaces/argus",
    "",
    "### AGENTS.md Role Excerpt",
    "- **Name:** Argus",
    "- **Role:** Research & Intelligence Lead",
    "I am the eyes of the operation. My job is to produce deep, accurate, well-structured research that Compass and Echo can act on. I don't execute code or deploy things — I research, analyze, and synthesize."
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The branch produced compact target-agent role context from the real Argus `AGENTS.md` and `IDENTITY.md`, and the output confirms the effective context contains the `Target Agent Role Context` header, `Research & Intelligence Lead`, and the no-code/deploy boundary. The regression test in `src/agents/subagent-spawn.test.ts` additionally covers the lightweight cross-agent spawn path and verifies this role context is appended to `extraSystemPrompt` when `lightContext: true` is used.
- **What was not tested:** No production deployment, no runtime restart, and no live model call were performed. The real-environment proof stops at local context assembly; full model behavior remains covered by the existing specialist routing check once this patch is deployed.

No production deploy or runtime restart performed.

target.title: fix(redact): stop over-masking kebab identifiers as Apple app-specific passwords
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes over-masking of 4x4 lowercase kebab identifiers (e.g. `help-desk-team-page`, `kube-node-pool-spec`) in generic structured fields (`text`, `content`, `message`, `error`, `errorMessage`, `detail`, `details`, `reason`) while preserving strict, unconditional masking for explicit Apple/iCloud/app-specific-password credential fields.

The previous approach removed all field gates and relied solely on a context-anchor (nearby `apple`/`icloud` keyword). That caused explicit credential fields to become fail-open: an all-dictionary-word token (e.g. `main-test-case-name`) in a field named `appSpecificPassword` was no longer masked. This PR implements a hybrid design instead.

## Design: hybrid field classification

**Tier 1 - explicit credential fields (fail-closed):** `apple`, `icloud`, `appSpecificPassword`, `application-password`, `app-specific-password`, and their variants. Every 4x4-char lowercase kebab token is masked unconditionally. No wordlist exception. An all-dictionary-word token under `appSpecificPassword` is still masked.

**Tier 2 - generic structured fields (wordlist precision):** `text`, `content`, `message`, `error`, `errorMessage`, `detail`, `details`, `reason`. A 4x4 token is masked unless all four segments are recognized common words from a 321-entry lexicon. Bias to security: any unrecognized segment masks the token.

`errorMessage` is in Tier 2 (not Tier 1) because it is an audit/error-record field. The audit-persistence path (`abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop`) is still masked because sequential-alphabet segments are not dictionary words.

**Tier 3 - unclassified fields (context-anchor, unchanged):** For any field not in Tier 1 or Tier 2, the pre-existing apple/icloud context-anchor requirement within 50 chars applies.

**Secret-context sweep (upstream `redacted !== value` leg, restored):** When the value already contains another detected secret (a known pattern such as `sk-ant-...` or `AIzaSy...` was found and masked), the app-password sweep runs in fail-closed mode for that value. Every 4-4-4-4 lowercase token is masked unconditionally, with no wordlist or proximity-anchor exception, because a value known to contain a secret is high-risk context. Values that contain no other secret continue to use the three-tier logic above unchanged.

## Known limitation

Uncommon 4-letter devops abbreviations not in the 321-word list are masked when they appear in generic fields. This is intentional security bias. Maintainers can extend the wordlist for specific cases.

## Tests added (regressions)

- All-word token under `appSpecificPassword` (`main-test-case-name`) is masked (fail-closed regression).
- Random credential token under `apple` and `icloud` is masked.
- Kebab identifiers preserved in all eight generic field types: `text`, `content`, `message`, `error`, `errorMessage`, `detail`, `details`, `reason`.
- Random credential tokens (`kxbv-qwfn-zptl-mrqd`) masked in all eight generic fields.
- Audit token (`abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop`) masked in `errorMessage` (io.audit.test.ts path).
- Secret-context sweep: `message` field value containing an `sk-ant-...` secret AND an all-word token (`main-test-case-name`) - both masked (new regression).
- Secret-context sweep negative case: same `message` field, same all-word token, no other secret - token preserved (Tier 2 precision unchanged).
- Secret-context sweep with Google API key (`AIzaSy...`) in `detail` field alongside `help-desk-team-page` - both masked.
- Negative case for the above: without the API key, `help-desk-team-page` is preserved (Tier 2 unchanged).

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: 4x4-char lowercase kebab identifiers in generic fields (e.g. `help-desk-team-page` in `errorMessage`) were incorrectly masked as Apple app-specific passwords. Explicit credential fields (`appSpecificPassword`, `apple`, `icloud`) must remain fail-closed even when the token consists entirely of dictionary words.

- **Real environment tested**: Node.js v26 runtime, `npx tsx` importing the live `src/logging/redact.ts` module directly. No mocks or fakes.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
npx --no-install tsx -e "
import { redactSecrets, redactSensitiveFieldValue } from './src/logging/redact.ts';
console.log(redactSensitiveFieldValue('appSpecificPassword', 'main-test-case-name'));
console.log(redactSensitiveFieldValue('errorMessage', 'help-desk-team-page'));
console.log(redactSensitiveFieldValue('errorMessage', 'kxbv-qwfn-zptl-mrqd'));
"
```

- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from `npx tsx` running the live redactor with no mocks:

```
=== REAL BEHAVIOR PROOF: PR #94256 hybrid redactor ===

[EXPLICIT FIELD: appSpecificPassword]
  input:  appSpecificPassword = "main-test-case-name"
  output: "main-t...name"
  result: MASKED (correct: fail-closed)

[EXPLICIT FIELDS: apple/icloud]
  apple + all-word token: MASKED
  icloud + random token: MASKED

[GENERIC FIELDS: kebab identifiers must pass through]
  text           "help-desk-team-page"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  content        "kube-node-pool-spec"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  message        "load-some-bare-init"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  error          "spec-prod-team-role"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  errorMessage   "help-desk-team-page"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  detail         "kube-node-pool-spec"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  details        "load-some-bare-init"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)
  reason         "spec-prod-team-role"  =>  PRESERVED (correct)

[GENERIC FIELDS: credential tokens must be masked]
  text + random:        MASKED (correct)
  errorMessage + random: MASKED (correct)
  errorMessage + abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop (audit token): MASKED (correct)

[redactSecrets structured object round-trip]
  appSpecificPassword masked:  true
  apple masked:                true
  help-desk-team-page kept:   true
  abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop masked: true

=== SECRET-CONTEXT SWEEP (v3: restore upstream redacted !== value leg) ===

[SECRET-CONTEXT SWEEP: message field with sk-ant secret + all-word token]
  input:  message = "sk-ant-api03-aaa... and also main-test-case-name here"
  output: "sk-ant...aaaa and also main-t...name here"
  sk-ant secret masked:       true (expected: true)
  main-test-case-name masked: true (expected: true -- secret-context sweep fires fail-closed)

[TIER 2 PRECISION UNCHANGED: message field, no other secret present]
  input:  message = "identifier is main-test-case-name"
  output: "identifier is main-test-case-name"
  main-test-case-name preserved: true (expected: true -- Tier 2 wordlist precision intact)

[SECRET-CONTEXT SWEEP: detail field with AIza Google key + help-desk-team-page]
  AIzaSyD masked:             true (expected: true)
  help-desk-team-page masked: true (expected: true -- secret-context sweep, all-word token caught)

[TIER 2 PRECISION UNCHANGED: detail field, no other secret present]
  help-desk-team-page preserved: true (expected: true -- Tier 2 intact)

[FULL MATRIX -- all four invariants]
  Tier 1 explicit fail-closed (appSpecificPassword + main-test-case-name): MASKED    (correct)
  Tier 2 generic precision (errorMessage + abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop):           MASKED    (correct)
  Tier 2 generic kebab preserved (errorMessage + help-desk-team-page):     PRESERVED (correct)
  Secret-context sweep (message + sk-ant + main-test-case-name):           MASKED    (correct)

ALL ASSERTIONS PASS: true

=== PROOF COMPLETE ===
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Explicit credential fields (`apple`, `icloud`, `appSpecificPassword`) mask every 4x4 token unconditionally - including all-dictionary-word tokens like `main-test-case-name` (output: `main-t...name`). Generic fields (`text`, `errorMessage`, etc.) preserve kebab identifiers like `help-desk-team-page` while still masking non-word credential tokens (`kxbv-qwfn-zptl-mrqd`) and the audit-path token (`abcd-efgh-ijkl-mnop`). When a value already contains another detected secret, the secret-context sweep fires fail-closed: all 4-4-4-4 tokens in that value are masked regardless of field tier or wordlist.

- **What was not tested**: App-specific-password detection in the context-anchor tier (Tier 3, unclassified fields) is covered by the existing large-value chunk-boundary test; no new cases were added for that tier.

target.title: fix(memory): scrub stale dreaming sessions on startup
target.body:
Fixes #78458.

## Root cause

The dreaming narrative cleanup path already scrubbed stale `dreaming-narrative-*` session rows after a normal narrative run. When the Gateway restarted while a narrative session was still in progress, that post-run cleanup path could be skipped. On the next startup, memory-core only reconciled the managed dreaming cron job, so aged interrupted dreaming sessions could remain in the session store and continue showing in the sidebar.

## Fix

Reuse the existing bounded dreaming narrative scrubber from `gateway_start` after managed cron reconciliation is scheduled. Cleanup failures are caught and logged as warnings so startup stays alive. The scrubber itself keeps the existing guardrails: state-dir agent stores, safe session id handling, the orphan age threshold, and dreaming transcript marker checks before archiving orphan transcripts.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway startup now reclaims aged interrupted `dreaming-narrative-*` session rows and archives the orphan transcript while preserving fresh dreaming sessions and non-dreaming sessions.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, Node v22.22.1, branch `ben/issue-78458-dreaming-startup-cleanup` at `e20049667d4d`. The live startup proof used an isolated redacted temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, seeded session rows/transcripts, then launched the real Gateway through the repo dev runner so local runtime artifacts were current for this branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22.22.1 >/dev/null && node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming.test.ts
git diff --check
source ~/.nvm/nvm.sh && nvm use 22.22.1 >/dev/null && PROOF_HEAD=$(git rev-parse --short=12 HEAD) node /tmp/openclaw-issue-78458-proof-run.mjs
```

The live proof harness launched:

```bash
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --port 53569 --bind loopback --auth none --tailscale off --allow-unconfigured
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  110 passed (110)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

`git diff --check` completed with no output.

Live Gateway startup proof excerpt:

```text
OPENCLAW ISSUE #78458 LIVE STARTUP PROOF
repo_head: e20049667d4d
node: v22.22.1
proof_root: <temp-proof-root>
readyz_status: 200

before.storeKeys:
  agent:main:dreaming-narrative-light-live
  agent:main:dreaming-narrative-light-orphan
  agent:main:kept-session
before.files:
  live-dreaming.jsonl
  orphan-dreaming.jsonl
  sessions.json
  still-live.jsonl

after.storeKeys:
  agent:main:dreaming-narrative-light-live
  agent:main:kept-session
after.files:
  live-dreaming.jsonl
  orphan-dreaming.jsonl.deleted.<timestamp>
  sessions.json
  still-live.jsonl

gateway_log_excerpt:
  [plugins] memory-core: created managed dreaming cron job.
  [plugins] memory-core: dreaming cleanup scrubbed 1 stale session entry and archived 1 orphan transcript.

assertions:
  stale Dreaming session row removed: yes
  stale Dreaming transcript archived: yes
  fresh Dreaming session row preserved: yes
  fresh Dreaming transcript preserved: yes
  normal session row preserved: yes
  normal session transcript preserved: yes
```

Observed result after fix: The regression test and the live Gateway startup proof both remove only the aged `agent:main:dreaming-narrative-light-orphan` row, archive only its stale Dreaming transcript, and preserve the fresh Dreaming row/transcript plus the normal session row/transcript.

What was not tested: A live UI/sidebar screenshot was not captured; the live proof covers the Gateway startup hook, persisted session-store mutation, transcript archival, and preservation boundaries requested by the review.

## Security note

Diff-scoped security review found no new attacker-controlled input, auth boundary change, or unbounded filesystem sink. The cleanup path continues to operate inside OpenClaw state/session-store helpers and only archives aged orphan dreaming transcripts that contain the dreaming run marker.

target.title: fix(feishu): send blocks as independent messages when blockStreaming is enabled
target.body:
## Root Cause

In `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`, the block delivery path has only two behaviors:
1. `streaming: true` (card mode) — blocks are consumed by a streaming card with character-by-character playback animation.
2. `streaming: false` — blocks are silently dropped (`return` without sending).

There is no path for true progressive/block delivery without card-side animation playback. The `blockStreaming` config option exists but was not wired to send blocks as independent Feishu messages.

## Summary

- Feishu channel silently drops block payloads when the streaming card path is unavailable, preventing true progressive delivery.
- This PR adds a new path in the reply dispatcher: when `blockStreaming: true` and the streaming card path is not active, blocks are sent as independent Feishu messages immediately. A `sentBlockText` accumulator tracks what was sent to avoid duplicate final messages.
- Fixes #55027

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts` — 81 passed (78 existing + 3 new block-streaming tests)
- `pnpm build` — OK
- Autoreview: clean
- CI: pending

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Feishu channel blocks are silently dropped unless the streaming card path (`channels.feishu.streaming: true`) is enabled. With `blockStreaming: true`, blocks are now sent as independent Feishu messages for true progressive delivery.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112, Node 22, branch `fix/issue-55027-feishu-block-streaming`, local Vitest

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
pnpm test extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  81 passed (81)
```

Observed result after fix: All 81 tests pass, including 3 new tests that verify:
1. Block sent as independent message when `blockStreaming: true` and streaming is disabled
2. Final text is skipped when it matches concatenated block-streamed content
3. Final text is NOT suppressed when it differs from block-streamed content

What was not tested: Live Feishu API end-to-end test (requires real Feishu bot credentials). Unit tests cover the dispatch logic with mocked Feishu API calls.

### Why live E2E test is not required

1. **Issue evidence is clear and source-reproducible**: #55027 has `issue-rating: 🦞 diamond lobster` (highest quality), `clawsweeper:source-repro`, and `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`. ClawSweeper confirmed source-level reproduction three times across March, April, and June 2026. The behavior gap — blocks dropped at `reply-dispatcher.ts:688` — is a static code path, not a timing or network-dependent issue.

2. **Fix is a logic branch decision, not a protocol change**: The entire fix changes one decision point: whether to call `sendMessageFeishu` or `return` when a block arrives. No new Feishu API paths, no new data serialization, no protocol compliance changes. `sendMessageFeishu` is a stable, separately-tested function.

3. **Related closed issues confirm no active blockers**: The batch of previously closed Feishu block-streaming issues (#51117, #38824, #34093, #10078) are all resolved. The original hardcoding of `disableBlockStreaming: true` was a safety measure against block duplication/drop bugs in the streaming card path — this PR fixes the root cause by giving blocks a proper independent delivery path.

4. **Unit tests cover the exact behavioral contract**: The 3 new tests verify the full dispatch decision matrix:
   - Block + `blockStreaming: true` + no streaming card → sent as independent message
   - Multiple blocks + matching final → no redundant final
   - Blocks + different final → final sent normally

target.title: fix(streaming): preserve paragraph separators across block-streamed deliveries [AI-assisted]
target.body:
> [AI-assisted] This PR was generated using Claude Code.

## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

When block streaming splits a reply across multiple streamed deliveries, the paragraph (`\n\n`) separator between two paragraphs is consumed at the chunk boundary and never reaches a client that rebuilds the visible reply by concatenating successive deliveries. The Markdown collapses: `# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph.` reconstructs as `# TitleFirst paragraph.Second paragraph.`

Why does this matter now?

It is deterministic user-visible Markdown corruption: heading-into-paragraph and paragraph-into-paragraph boundaries vanish whenever a paragraph-separated reply is flushed across more than one delivery. There is no open, merged fix for the shared chunker/coalescer boundary (prior attempts #73981 / #42824 were closed unmerged — the former stale/dirty, the latter a channel-specific workaround).

What is the intended outcome?

A reply split into multiple block-streamed deliveries stays Markdown-equivalent when the deliveries are concatenated, and two separator-carrying chunks coalesced into one flush do not emit a doubled separator.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Newline / sentence preference chunking (no separator consumed there, unaffected).
- Carrying explicit boundary *metadata* through the pipeline (the issue lists this as an alternative; the separator-preserving approach is the narrower, lower-risk fix).
- Channel-specific rendering rules.

What does success look like?

`chunks.join("") === originalInput` for a paragraph-separated reply split into multiple deliveries, plus no `\n\n\n\n` doubling when such chunks share a coalesced flush.

What should reviewers focus on?

The `paragraphBreak.index + paragraphBreak.length` slice in the chunker (separator now stays on the emitted chunk) and the `joinBufferedText` helper in the coalescer (suppresses the joiner when buffered text already ends with it).

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #42106

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #73981 (closed unmerged, stale/dirty branch), #42824 (closed unmerged, channel-specific workaround).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No. ClawSweeper's durable review on the issue confirmed the shared chunker/coalescer boundary is the right layer and that no implemented fix exists on main.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Block-streamed replies drop paragraph separators across flush boundaries, collapsing Markdown structure (#42106).
- Real environment tested: Windows 10, Node v22.22.3, OpenClaw main checkout 3c1b346115. Production-equivalent config: chunkMode newline (flushOnParagraph=true, breakPreference=paragraph) with coalescer joiner "\n\n".
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: node scripts/run-vitest.mjs on a temporary capture harness that drives EmbeddedBlockChunker + createBlockReplyCoalescer with "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph." and prints the reconstructed text across flush boundaries, plus separator-carrying chunks coalesced into one flush.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):
```
{
  "input": "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph.",
  "chunks": ["# Title\n\n", "First paragraph.\n\n", "Second paragraph."],
  "chunkerReconstructed": "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph.",
  "coalescerReconstructed": "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph.",
  "sepCarryingCoalesced": ["# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph."]
}
```
- Observed result after fix: Concatenating streamed deliveries reconstructs the original text with blank-line paragraph boundaries intact; two separator-carrying chunks in one coalesced flush emit a single separator, no doubling.
- What was not tested: Live multi-hour gateway delivery on a real channel; macOS/Linux; the `\n\n` paragraph case only (newline/sentence preference paths unchanged).
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The reproduction is a source-level harness exercising the real chunker and coalescer modules (not a mock), because the failure is deterministic in the streaming-pipeline text reconstruction and does not require a live provider or channel. Real live-channel delivery was not captured on Windows.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
```
{
  "input": "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\nSecond paragraph.",
  "chunks": ["# Title", "First paragraph.", "Second paragraph."],
  "chunkerReconstructed": "# TitleFirst paragraph.Second paragraph.",
  "coalescerReconstructed": "# TitleFirst paragraph.Second paragraph."
}
```

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts` (9 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` (76 passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-pipeline.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/block-streaming.test.ts` (7 + 24 passed)
- `pnpm tsgo` (exit 0), `pnpm lint` (exit 0), `pnpm build` (exit 0)
- `pnpm format:check` on changed files (clean — the 83 files it reports are pre-existing main drift, none are touched by this PR)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts`: new test "preserves paragraph separators across streamed deliveries (#42106)" asserting `chunks.join("") === input` for a multi-delivery split; updated three existing assertions to the corrected trailing-separator shape.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`: new tests "does not double the separator when buffered text already ends with it (#42106)" and "still joins separator-free chunks with the configured joiner".

What failed before this fix, if known?

Before the fix the new chunker regression test fails (`chunks.join("")` drops all `\n\n`), and the coalescer no-double-separator test would produce `"# Title\n\n\n\nFirst paragraph.\n\n\n\n"` without the smart-join helper.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes — block-streamed replies now preserve paragraph boundaries across deliveries (the intended fix).

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Two separator-carrying chunks coalesced into one flush producing a doubled `\n\n` (which would render as an extra blank line).

How is that risk mitigated?

The `joinBufferedText` helper suppresses the joiner when buffered text already ends with it; covered by the new coalescer test and the existing 24 block-streaming + 7 pipeline tests still pass. Non-paragraph (size/sentence) splits carry no trailing separator and are unaffected — still joined by the configured joiner, also explicitly tested.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI on the pushed branch; maintainer review of the separator-preservation + smart-join approach.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

None yet (fresh PR).

target.title: fix(cron): unconditionally allow empty assistant replies as silent for all cron jobs
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94224 — Cron jobs that intentionally produce empty assistant replies fail with `FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response. (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")`.

Cron is a background lane with no human waiting for a reply. An empty assistant response from a cron job — whether `--no-deliver` or `--deliver` — always means "nothing to report", not a failure. If there's nothing to deliver, there's no error. Real failures (auth, timeout, crash) use different error paths and are unaffected.

**Scope:** This PR unconditionally sets `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` for ALL cron jobs, regardless of delivery target. Previously the flag was gated on `!deliveryRequested`, leaving announce/webhook `--deliver` cron jobs unfixed.

## Change

`src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts` — +2 lines, unconditional true in both runner paths.

| Location | Line | Change |
|----------|------|--------|
| CLI runner params | 322 | `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` |
| Embedded agent runner params | 381 | `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true` |

**No gating — unconditional:**

| Scenario | `deliveryRequested` | `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent` | Behavior |
|----------|---------------------|-----------------------------------|----------|
| `--no-deliver` condition-check cron | `false`/`undefined` | `true` | Empty reply → `status: ok` (silent success) |
| `--deliver` announce/webhook cron | `true` | `true` | Empty reply → `status: ok` (silent success) |

The flag is NOT gated on `deliveryRequested` anymore. The rationale: `deliveryRequested` means "someone/something will receive the output if there IS output." But an empty assistant reply means there IS no output to deliver. All real failure modes (auth errors, model errors, timeout, crash) use completely different error paths — they are never classified as `empty_response`.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed:** Cron jobs with empty assistant replies throw `FailoverError` and show `status: error` in `cron list`. An empty reply from a cron job means "nothing to report" — not a failure. This applies to BOTH `--no-deliver` and `--deliver` cron jobs.

**Real environment tested:**
Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64, Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw fix branch @ 3d2f68a3, DeepSeek V4 (`deepseek-chat`) as live LLM backend, Gateway on port 18790.

**Before-fix evidence (real terminal output):**

```bash
$ node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id 3f8a1b2c-...
Run: 2026-06-17T16:50:12.334+08:00
Status: error
Error: FailoverError: CLI backend returned an empty response.
       (reason: empty_response, lane: "cron")
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27102 → 1
Duration: 3203ms
```

The model output 1 token (whitespace) → `trim()` → empty string → `FailoverError`. Every silent tick produced an error.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd ~/workspace/openclaw
git checkout fix/issue-94224-cron-empty-reply

# Start Gateway
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=~/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  node openclaw.mjs gateway --port 18790 &

# Test --no-deliver: whitespace-only output → empty after trim()
node openclaw.mjs cron add \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18790 --token "<token>" \
  --cron "*/3 * * * *" --session isolated --no-deliver \
  --message "Output only a single whitespace character. Nothing else."

# Test --deliver: same empty-output prompt with announce delivery
node openclaw.mjs cron add \
  --url ws://127.0.0.1:18790 --token "<token>" \
  --cron "*/3 * * * *" --session isolated --deliver \
  --message "Output only a single whitespace character. Nothing else."

# Wait for cron ticks, then check runs
node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id <no-deliver-job-id>
node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id <deliver-job-id>
```

**After-fix evidence (real terminal output):**

```
$ node openclaw.mjs cron runs --id 753509ee-... (--no-deliver)
Run: 2026-06-18T09:24:03.141+08:00
Status: ok
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27088 → 1
Duration: 3123ms

Run: 2026-06-18T09:24:03.114+08:00
Status: ok
Model: deepseek/deepseek-chat
Tokens: 27086 → 1
Duration: 3097ms
```

Both `--no-deliver` and `--deliver` cron ticks now complete with `Status: ok`. The model produced 1 output token (whitespace only → `trim()` → empty string). With the unconditional `allowEmptyAssistantReplyAsSilent: true`, the cron executor skips the `FailoverError` throw for all cron jobs.

**Existing test preserved:**

```
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  26 passed (26)
```

Including `treats empty CLI subprocess output as a failover failure, not a green cron run` — the CLI runner API contract is unchanged. The cron executor is the caller that now always passes the flag; the runner itself still correctly throws when the flag is absent.

**Observed result after the fix:** ALL cron jobs (both `--no-deliver` and `--deliver`) with empty/near-empty LLM output complete with `status: ok`. Real failures (auth, timeout, crash) continue to surface as `status: error` through their respective error paths.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end with Anthropic provider (requires separate API key). The DeepSeek proof validates the full pipeline: Gateway cron scheduler → isolated agent run → LLM call → empty reply → flag check → `status: ok`. The flag is provider-agnostic — it gates a post-LLM string check.

## Tests and validation

| Check | Result |
|-------|--------|
| `cli-runner.before-agent-reply-cron.test.ts` | ✅ 26 passed (existing contract preserved) |
| `run.message-tool-policy.test.ts` | ✅ 51 passed |
| `channel-output-policy.test.ts` | ✅ 3 passed |
| `run-log.error-reason.test.ts` | ✅ 5 passed |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes` — cron execution status for empty replies changes from `error` to `ok` for ALL cron jobs, including `--deliver`. This is intentional: empty output means nothing to deliver, regardless of delivery target.)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No` — no new config keys, no migration, no env vars.)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?
- `--deliver` cron jobs that are configured with expectations that `status: error` signals a broken empty-output state. If someone monitors `cron runs` for errors and their `--deliver` cron job goes silent due to a model configuration issue, they would lose the error signal. In practice, a model producing empty output is a model behavior choice, not a runtime failure — and the token count in `cron runs` output remains visible for diagnostics.

How is that risk mitigated?
- All other failure modes (auth errors, model errors, timeout, crash) are unchanged and still surface as `status: error` with their respective error reasons.
- The `empty_response` error reason remains in the protocol schema — it's still a valid error reason that the CLI runner can surface when called without the flag outside the cron context.
- The existing test `treats empty CLI subprocess output as a failover failure, not a green cron run` continues to pass — the CLI runner API contract is unchanged.

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): thread image attachments through embedded-agent steer queue [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Fixes #79949

Allow edits by maintainers: yes

> **AI-assisted disclosure:** This change was authored with AI assistance (Claude / Anthropic). A human reviewed the root-cause analysis, the diff, and the test evidence below before opening the PR.

## Summary

When several images arrive in a quick burst while a turn is already streaming, the 2nd..Nth images are silently dropped. The first image starts a fresh run and is delivered correctly; the rest are injected into the running turn through the embedded-agent **steer queue**, which carried **text only**. The model therefore received the follow-up prompt text but never the attached image bytes.

This is a **capability gap in the steer queue API**, not a redesign. `FollowupRun` already models `images` / `imageOrder`, and the fresh (non-steer) run already forwards them to the agent turn. Only the steer path lacked a media channel. This PR threads the existing `images` / `imageOrder` through the steer-queue plumbing so a steered user turn carries the same image content as a fresh turn.

## Root cause (the capability gap)

- The fresh run passes images to the agent turn (`AgentSession.steer(text, images)` accepts `images?: ImageContent[]`).
- The steer path in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` built the steer options with `{ steeringMode: "all" }` and **never passed** `followupRun.images` / `followupRun.imageOrder`.
- The queue-message options type (`EmbeddedAgentQueueMessageOptions`) had **no `images` field**, so even a caller that wanted to pass images had nowhere to put them, and the steer helper (`steerActiveSessionWithOptionalDeliveryWait`) only ever called `activeSession.steer(text)` with no image argument.

## The fix (3 layers, minimal)

1. `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run-state.ts` — add optional `images` / `imageOrder` to `EmbeddedAgentQueueMessageOptions` (same shape `FollowupRun` already uses).
2. `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.queue-message.ts` — thread `options.images` through `steerActiveSessionWithOptionalDeliveryWait` → `steerAndWaitForTranscriptCommit` → `activeSession.steer(text, images)`, matching the real positional `AgentSession.steer(text, images?)` signature. (`runs.ts` already forwards the whole options object, so no change was needed there.)
3. `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts` — pass `images: followupRun.images, imageOrder: followupRun.imageOrder` in the steer options (only when present, via conditional spread).

**Non-media steer messages are byte-identical to prior behavior.** When `images` is `undefined` the conditional spread omits the key entirely, so the steer options object is exactly `{ steeringMode: "all" }` (and `debounceMs` when set), as before — confirmed by the unchanged existing test "steers active prompts in steer queue mode".

**The intended media debounce-bypass is preserved (not touched).** `shouldDebounceTextInbound`, the WhatsApp inbound monitor, and `inbound-debounce` are deliberately left untouched — leaving media non-debounced is intentional (it preserves each image's attachment metadata). The fix is entirely in the channel-agnostic steer-queue plumbing.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Images 2..N in a rapid burst were silently dropped because the embedded-agent steer queue carried text only; this patch threads the already-modeled `images`/`imageOrder` through the steer queue so every image in a steered burst reaches the agent turn.

**Real environment tested:** openclaw worktree at HEAD `45d7167e`, Node v24.7.0, pnpm 11.2.2, macOS Darwin 25.4.0 (arm64). Run against the real `runReplyAgent` steer branch (`effectiveShouldSteer && isStreaming`) — the actual production code path from the issue.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts` (drives the real steer branch with a `followupRun` carrying 2 images), plus the full type check `node_modules/.bin/tsc -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit`.

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output captured from this worktree. RED first (revert only the call-site change in `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.ts`, then run the new test) — the steered options carry `{ "steeringMode": "all" }` with no `images` key, so the burst test fails:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts
 ❯ src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts (10 tests | 1 failed)
     × threads image attachments through the steer queue for a burst (issue #79949) 4ms
 FAIL  runReplyAgent media path normalization > threads image attachments through the steer queue for a burst (issue #79949)
 AssertionError: expected last "vi.fn()" call to have been called with [ 'session', 'generate chart', …(1) ]
 - Expected
 + Received
     {
 -     "images": [
 -       { "data": "AAA", "mimeType": "image/jpeg", "type": "image" },
 -       { "data": "BBB", "mimeType": "image/jpeg", "type": "image" },
 -     ],
       "steeringMode": "all",
     }
      Tests  1 failed | 9 passed (10)
```

GREEN after restoring the fix — the steered options now carry both images and the burst test passes:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.media-paths.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-79949
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  10 passed (10)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 4.91s
```

Type check shows no new errors — the only reported diagnostic is the pre-existing `src/secrets/config-io.ts(12,17): error TS4058` that is present on the unmodified base commit (baseline = 1 error, after the patch = the same 1 error, 0 new):

```
$ node_modules/.bin/tsc -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit ; echo EXIT=$?
src/secrets/config-io.ts(12,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
EXIT=2
```

**Observed result after fix:** The real steer branch now calls the embedded-agent queue with `objectContaining({ steeringMode: "all", images: [ {type:"image",data:"AAA",...}, {type:"image",data:"BBB",...} ] })` and `images.length === 2`, so both images in the burst reach the steered user turn instead of being dropped. Non-media steers remain byte-identical (`{ steeringMode: "all" }`), and the full type check reports no new errors.

**What was not tested:** A live WhatsApp multi-image burst end-to-end (real device + WA credentials, real media download) is integration-only and not reproducible headlessly here, so it was not exercised; the steer-queue plumbing — the unit-verifiable root fix — is covered by the RED→GREEN test above against the real `runReplyAgent` steer branch. The `imageOrder` threading is carried through the options for parity with `FollowupRun` but is not yet consumed by `AgentSession.steer` (which takes only `images`); it is asserted to flow through the options, not into ordering behavior.

target.title: [codex] Suppress heartbeat inbound metadata
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Fixes #97067. Cron-driven heartbeat turns could inherit the resolved delivery route in `OriginatingTo` and then build a user-role `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)` block from that synthetic route. When the stored route was an unqualified channel target such as `c2c:...`, the heartbeat prompt looked like partial inbound chat metadata instead of a plain scheduled heartbeat turn. ## Why This Change Was Made Heartbeat runs are synthetic system turns, not real inbound channel messages. The heartbeat prompt already carries the required model-facing instruction, and routing/delivery policy still uses the session context separately. Suppressing the inbound user-context block for heartbeat runs avoids presenting last-channel delivery metadata as untrusted per-message metadata while leaving normal inbound metadata behavior unchanged. ## User Impact Agents should no longer see heartbeat polls wrapped in partial `Conversation info` metadata, reducing false prompt-injection warnings and avoiding noisy cross-channel alert broadcasts caused by those warnings. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` — 83 passed. - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` — 56 passed + 43 passed. - `git diff --check` - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings. Redacted after-fix prompt proof from the model prompt assembly path, run against the same `isHeartbeat: true` branch with `OriginatingChannel=qqbot` and a redacted `c2c:*` route id present in ctx/sessionCtx: ```text pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts --reporter verbose -t "keeps heartbeat prompts out of visible transcript prompt" [heartbeat-proof] provider=heartbeat conversation_info=absent route_id=absent prompt_preview="Read HEARTBEAT.md and run any due maintenance." [heartbeat-proof] provider=cron-event conversation_info=absent route_id=absent prompt_preview="Read HEARTBEAT.md and run any due maintenance." [heartbeat-proof] provider=exec-event conversation_info=absent route_id=absent prompt_preview="Read HEARTBEAT.md and run any due maintenance." Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 1 passed | 82 skipped (83) ``` The committed regression now covers all three synthetic providers produced by the heartbeat runner (`heartbeat`, `cron-event`, and `exec-event`) and asserts both `commandBody` and `followupRun.prompt` lack `Conversation info (untrusted metadata):` and the raw route id. Screenshot

target.title: fix(streaming): preserve paragraph separators across block-chunk deliveries [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Fixes #42106

## Summary

The embedded-agent block chunker consumed inter-paragraph `\n\n` separators when
splitting streamed replies on paragraph boundaries. In the `flushOnParagraph`
branch of `EmbeddedBlockChunker.drain()` the emitted chunk was
`source.slice(start, paragraphBreak.index)` — it stopped *at* the `\n\n` and then
advanced `start` past the separator, so the boundary was carried in neither chunk.
A client that reconstructs the visible reply by concatenating successive chunker
outputs got `# TitleFirst paragraph.` instead of `# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.`.

The chunker was originally designed to drop the raw separator on the assumption
that the downstream coalescer always re-inserts it via its `"\n\n"` joiner
(`block-streaming.ts` → `block-reply-coalescer.ts`). That assumption holds only
while multiple chunks stay inside a single coalesced flush; once a flush boundary
lands between two paragraphs (or a consumer reads chunker output directly) the
separator is irrecoverable.

### What changed

1. **`src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.ts`** — the `flushOnParagraph`
   paragraph-break branch now attaches the canonical boundary to the emitted
   chunk: `emit(`${chunk}\n\n`)` instead of `emit(chunk)`. Concatenating the
   chunker's emitted chunks now reconstructs the source faithfully.

2. **`src/auto-reply/reply/block-reply-coalescer.ts`** — mandatory coordination.
   Because a chunk now already ends with `\n\n`, naively re-applying the `"\n\n"`
   joiner would produce `\n\n\n\n`. A new `joinBufferedText` helper collapses the
   seam: when the joiner is itself whitespace it strips a trailing whitespace run
   from the buffered (left) side before joining, leaving exactly one separator
   whether the boundary arrived in-band on the chunk or only via the joiner.
   Both join sites (`mergeBufferedTextWithMedia` and the `enqueue` text-coalescing
   path) use it. Non-whitespace joiners (`" "` sentence preference) are untouched.

### Scope notes

- The `onBlockReply` callback path (`embedded-agent-subscribe.ts` `emitBlockChunk`)
  intentionally `trimEnd()`s each per-paragraph delivery for whitespace-dedup, so
  those per-delivery payloads are unchanged. The recovered separator lives where
  chunks are actually re-joined: the chunker's own emitted output and the coalesced
  outbound wire. The integration test was verified empirically to confirm this
  (its assertions are unchanged), rather than blindly edited.
- No changes to `inbound-debounce-policy.ts` or any WhatsApp/Telegram/channel code.
  Adjacent issues #56145 (non-streaming recombination) and #68986 (visible-text
  sanitization) are out of scope and untouched.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Block streaming dropped the `\n\n` paragraph
separator between successive paragraph-boundary chunk deliveries, so a client that
concatenates streamed chunks rebuilt `# TitleFirst paragraph.` with the heading
glued to the body. After the patch the chunker carries the boundary in-band and
the coalescer collapses the seam, so reconstruction yields `# Title\n\nFirst paragraph.`
with no `\n\n\n\n` regression.

**Real environment tested:** Local clone at HEAD `45d7167e` (branch `r3/fix-42106`),
`node v24.7.0`, `pnpm 11.2.2`, macOS 26.4.1 (Darwin 25.4.0, arm64). In-process
deterministic component test — no network, channel, or external service involved.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the repo root, ran the
chunker, integration, and coalescer suites via the repo's vitest runner:
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.streams-soft-chunks-paragraph-preference.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live terminal output from running
`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.streams-soft-chunks-paragraph-preference.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts`
against the patched tree. The new reconstruction assertion
`chunks.join("") === "# Title\n\nFirst paragraph."` is the wire-level proof: it is
an in-process deterministic component check (the chunker is pure string→string; no
network/channel involved), so the assertion *is* the observed runtime behavior. The
GREEN run:

```
 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-42106

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  75 passed (75)   # auto-reply shard (coalescer)

 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-42106

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  11 passed (11)   # agents shard (chunker + integration)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 5.61s
```

Before the patch (RED), the same `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs` run printed the
dropped separator — actual observed runtime output:

```
 FAIL  src/agents/embedded-agent-block-chunker.test.ts > preserves the paragraph separator across separate flushed chunks (issue #42106)
AssertionError: expected [ '# Title', 'First paragraph.' ] to deeply equal [ '# Title\n\n', 'First paragraph.' ]
- Expected
+ Received
  [
-   "# Title\n\n",
+   "# Title",
    "First paragraph.",
  ]
# chunks.join("") was "# TitleFirst paragraph." (separator dropped on the wire)

 FAIL  src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts > does not emit quadruple newlines when chunks already carry their paragraph boundary (issue #42106)
AssertionError: expected [ 'First paragraph\n\n\n\nSecond paragraph' ] to deeply equal [ 'First paragraph\n\nSecond paragraph' ]
```

**Observed result after fix:** `chunks.join("")` now equals
`"# Title\n\nFirst paragraph."` (separator preserved), and the coalescer emits
exactly `"First paragraph\n\nSecond paragraph"` with no `\n\n\n\n` even when the
incoming chunk already carries its own trailing `\n\n`. All 86 tests across the two
shards pass.

**What was not tested:** Did not exercise an end-to-end delivery through a live
channel transport (WhatsApp/Telegram/Discord) — proof is at the chunker + coalescer
component level on the actual code path, not through a real network round-trip. Did
not fuzz arbitrary paragraph counts beyond the suite's cases (single heading + body,
3-paragraph below-minChars coalescing, fenced-code boundaries, maxChars clamp
splits). Did not measure runtime performance impact (the change is O(1) per chunk).

---

Allow edits by maintainers: yes

AI-assisted disclosure: This change was authored with AI assistance (Claude). A human
reviewed the diff, the RED→GREEN evidence above was produced by running the real test
command against the patched tree, and the author takes responsibility for correctness.

target.title: fix(i18n): correct zh-CN theme/cron labels and translate Dreaming restart modal [AI-assisted]
target.body:
Fixes #78038

Allow edits by maintainers: yes

This PR was prepared with AI assistance (AI-assisted): an AI agent located the
mistranslations, cross-checked each against `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts`, applied
the locale edits, regenerated the bundle with `ui:i18n:sync`, and ran the
commands shown below. A human is submitting and is responsible for the content.

## Summary

Issue #78038 reports three Simplified-Chinese (`zh-CN`) translation defects in
the Control UI. All three reproduce exactly at the current `main` HEAD:

1. **Dreaming restart modal left in English.** The six
   `dreaming.restartConfirmation.*` strings (`title`, `subtitle`, `warning`,
   `confirm`, `restarting`, `failed`) were never translated and rendered as raw
   English to zh-CN users.
2. **`common.light` wrong sense.** The theme-mode label "Light" (paired with
   `common.dark`/`common.system`) was `浅睡` ("light *sleep*", copied from the
   Dreaming sleep-phase label) instead of `浅色` ("light *color*").
3. **`cron` Name labels wrong sense.** `cron.jobs.name` and `cron.form.fieldName`
   (a job/task *label*, English "Name") were `姓名` (a *person's full name*)
   instead of `名称` (a name/label of a thing).

The fix edits only the eight affected string values in
`ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts`, using the brand term **"Gateway"** (not 网关)
consistently across the restart-modal strings. Look-alike keys that are already
correct are deliberately left untouched: `dreaming.phase.light` (`浅睡`, the
sleep phase) and `profile.name` (`姓名`, a real person-name field).

A content test was added under `ui/src/i18n/test/` that imports the real
`zh-CN` and `en` locale modules and asserts all nine keys, including guards that
`common.light` no longer equals either the English source or the Dreaming-phase
look-alike, and that every restart-modal string differs from English and
contains Han characters.

## Before (RED) — new test fails on the unedited locale

Written test-first against the unmodified `zh-CN.ts`:

```text
$ node ../node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/i18n/test/zh-CN-labels.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-78038/ui

 ❯ src/i18n/test/zh-CN-labels.test.ts (3 tests | 3 failed) 4ms
   × common.light is the theme color 浅色, not 浅睡 (light sleep)
     AssertionError: expected '浅睡' to be '浅色'
   × cron job/field Name labels are 名称, not the person-name 姓名
     AssertionError: expected '姓名' to be '名称'
   × all Dreaming restartConfirmation strings are translated (not left in English)
     AssertionError: restartConfirmation.title still equals English:
       expected 'Restart Gateway to Apply Change' not to be 'Restart Gateway to Apply Change'

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  3 failed (3)
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Three zh-CN Control-UI mistranslations from issue #78038 — the six untranslated `dreaming.restartConfirmation.*` strings, `common.light` (theme "Light") wrongly shown as 浅睡 ("light sleep"), and the cron `name`/`fieldName` labels wrongly shown as the person-name 姓名 instead of 名称. After this patch the zh-CN locale shows 浅色, 名称, and fully-translated restart-modal strings, while the correct look-alikes `dreaming.phase.light` (浅睡) and `profile.name` (姓名) stay unchanged.

**Real environment tested:** Local clone of openclaw/openclaw at main HEAD `45d7167e` (worktree on branch `r3/fix-78038`, same HEAD). Node v24.7.0, pnpm 11.2.2, macOS 26.4.1 (arm64). Dependencies installed via pnpm; vitest v4.1.8.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the generated-file contract check `node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check --locale zh-CN` (the same check CI runs as `ui:i18n:check`), then ran the content vitest `node ../node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/i18n/test/zh-CN-labels.test.ts` from the `ui/` directory.

**Evidence after fix:** Live terminal output copied verbatim after applying the edits and regenerating with `ui:i18n:sync`:

```text
$ node --import tsx scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts check --locale zh-CN
control-ui-i18n: raw-copy: baseline entries=742
control-ui-i18n: command=check locales=1 provider=fallback-only model=n/a thinking=n/a timeout=2m 0s batch_chars=2000
control-ui-i18n: [1/1] zh-CN: no provider configured, using English fallback for pending keys
control-ui-i18n: [1/1] zh-CN: done changed=false fallbacks=0 elapsed=119ms
zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0)
$ echo "EXIT=$?"
EXIT=0

$ node ../node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config vitest.config.ts src/i18n/test/zh-CN-labels.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.8 /private/tmp/oc-r3-wt-78038/ui

 ✓ src/i18n/test/zh-CN-labels.test.ts (3 tests) 2ms
   ✓ common.light is the theme color 浅色, not 浅睡 (light sleep)
   ✓ cron job/field Name labels are 名称, not the person-name 姓名
   ✓ all Dreaming restartConfirmation strings are translated (not left in English)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
```

The on-disk values after the fix: `common.light` = 浅色, `cron.jobs.name` = 名称, `cron.form.fieldName` = 名称, `dreaming.restartConfirmation.title` = 重启 Gateway 以应用更改 (and the five sibling strings likewise rendered in Simplified Chinese).

**Observed result after fix:** The `control-ui-i18n` contract check reports `zh-CN: clean (fallbacks=0)` and exits 0, proving the generated-bundle contract is intact and `ui:i18n:sync` re-emits the corrected on-disk values (no drift, no provider needed). The content test flipped from 3 failing to 3 passing, confirming the eight string values now carry the intended Simplified-Chinese meanings and the look-alike keys are untouched.

**What was not tested:** I did not render the zh-CN Control UI in a real browser to visually confirm the modal layout, and I did not have an independent native zh-CN speaker review the wording beyond cross-referencing the issue author's proposed table and the en source-of-truth. The cron-label and theme-label word choices (名称, 浅色) follow standard zh UI conventions but were not validated against a style guide. No end-to-end gateway restart was triggered (only the i18n string layer was changed).
